commit.message:
fix: restore GPU CI job (#23)

## Summary
- keep the GPU CI on the archived two-stage flow while removing the
stale bellman-cuda / CRS setup
- fix archived integration tests to resolve `test_fixtures` and
`artifacts/app.bin` from the runtime `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` that nextest
remaps on the GPU runner
- remove the stale `WORKSPACE_DIR` override from the archive build step
because it was not actually fixing archive replay

## Validation
- `cargo fmt -p ethereum_prover -- --check`
- `ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true cargo nextest list -p ethereum_prover
--test integration --release`
- `ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true cargo nextest archive --release -p
ethereum_prover --test integration --archive-file
prover-gpu-tests-local.tar.zst`
- `cargo nextest run --archive-file prover-gpu-tests-local.tar.zst
--workspace-remap "$(pwd)" cpu_witness_from_fixture_block`

## Context
The GPU CI failure was not missing fixture files in the workflow
checkout. The archived test binary was using compile-time
`env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")`, so after being built in the container it
still pointed at the container path when replayed on the GPU runner.
Switching the test helper to the runtime `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` makes the
archive replay pick up the remapped GPU-runner checkout path as
intended.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Igor Aleksanov <popzxc@yandex.ru>

repository.description: ZK prover for the Ethereum STF

commit.message:
feat(std): add support for custom extern function symbol names (#18)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Extern functions support a [[lang = "..."]] attribute to set the
emitted/link-time symbol (link_name).
  * New prelude APIs: assert(condition) and assert_eq(a, b).

* **Bug Fixes**
* Codegen now emits/looks up extern symbols by resolved link_name and
reuses existing declarations, avoiding duplicate or conflicting
declarations.

* **Documentation**
* Std library docs and prelude updated to document [[lang=...]] and
cstc_std_* runtime symbol mappings.

* **Tests**
* Added/extended tests covering extern link_name resolution and symbol
reuse.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

commit.message:
fix(mcp): silence ctx-hub startup output

Summary:
- make skill registration quiet by default during MCP startup
- add a regression test for silent registerSkillTools execution
- normalize manifest input types for interface reference generation

Rationale:
- ctx-hub was writing to stdout before initialize, which breaks the
  stdio MCP handshake
- optional logging keeps startup diagnostics available without
  polluting MCP responses

- node --test --test-concurrency=1 tests/skill-contracts.test.mjs
  tests/mcp-tool-registry.test.mjs tests/interface-reference.test.mjs

  capture and verify stdout stays empty

commit.message:
fix(web): honor DAO RPC ordering in wagmi config (#560)

Summary:
- normalize DAO chain metadata in a shared helper reused by the dapp
  provider
- build explicit viem transports from DAO-configured RPC URLs, keep
  their fallback order stable, and fail fast when none are configured
- avoid adding the built-in mainnet chain when the DAO itself already
  runs on chain id 1

Rationale:
- duplicate mainnet entries let RainbowKit and wagmi prefer built-in
  Ethereum RPC defaults over daoConfig.chain.rpcs
- explicit fallback transports preserve DAO RPC priority and fail over
  to the next configured endpoint when one RPC is unavailable
- empty RPC lists should fail with a clear DAO-chain error instead of
  deferring to an unusable transport with no URL

Tests:
- pnpm exec eslint src/config/wagmi.ts src/providers/dapp.provider.tsx
  src/components/members-table/hooks/useMembersData.ts
- pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
- pnpm dlx tsx -e ordered fallback transport probe
- pnpm dlx tsx -e empty RPC error probe
- curl -fsSL https://api.degov.ai/dao/config/fluence-dao?format=yml

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(capabilities): preserve extension approval boundaries

Summary:
- route extension-backed skill and workflow repairs to manual enable_extension actions instead of direct toggle actions

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: handle transient L1 RPC failures (#22)

## Summary
- add explicit retry helpers around L1 head and block-input RPC reads
- keep the continuous block stream alive on repeated L1 failures and
skip the block after retries instead of bubbling the error up and
shutting down the runtime
- reuse the same retry wrapper for single-block RPC fetches and make
EthProofs upload logs only report success on actual success
- checked the local Alloy 1.4.3 retry layer sources; the built-in policy
is centered on retryable transport/rate-limit responses, so
connection-timeout handling stays explicit at the block-stream boundary

## Testing
- `ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=1 cargo check -p ethereum_prover --lib`
- `RUST_MIN_STACK=16777216 ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=1 cargo test -p
ethereum_prover --lib -- --nocapture`
- `RUST_MIN_STACK=16777216 ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=1 cargo nextest run -p
ethereum_prover --lib`

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: restore multicall batching for DAO RPC reads (#559)

* fix(web): restore multicall batching for RPC reads

Summary:
- fix the shared multicall batch size to use viem's byte-based default
- configure wagmi transports from each chain's DAO RPC list via fallback
- make shared wagmi multicall batching explicit for public read traffic

Rationale:
- the previous batch size of 10 bytes split existing multicall reads into
  near-single-call requests
- RainbowKit's default transport setup only created one http transport per
  chain, which skipped the DAO RPC fallback list from config
- reducing fan-out at the shared client layer lowers pressure on public RPCs
  without rewriting each page-level hook

Tests:
- cd packages/web && pnpm lint
- cd packages/web && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
(cherry picked from commit cb724fb8759aa7ef9421e4e117be0313dfc6ee8c)

* fix(web): harden wagmi transport selection

Summary:
- preserve DAO RPC ordering by disabling viem fallback ranking
- dedupe configured chains and transports by chain id before creating wagmi
  config
- fail fast when a chain has no configured RPC URLs

Rationale:
- automated review surfaced edge cases that could silently reorder RPC
  selection or duplicate mainnet entries
- raising an explicit config error is easier to diagnose than a later
  request-time transport failure

Tests:
- cd packages/web && pnpm lint
- cd packages/web && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(orchestration): add hierarchy-aware task selection

Summary:
- add parent and child issue references to the shared tracker and
  normalized issue models
- extend the Linear query and normalization path to read parent and
  child issue metadata from the live children schema
- add orchestrator dispatch helpers and tests for blocker-aware and
  hierarchy-aware filtering plus leaf-first ordering
- update architecture, Linear, testing, and hierarchy reference docs

Rationale:
- parent issues must stay blocked until every child issue is terminal
  in the latest tracker snapshot
- the selection helpers can land before the full scheduler actor so
  COE-277 does not depend on inventing the rest of COE-268
- keeping hierarchy metadata in shared models makes future scheduler
  and snapshot integration deterministic

  opensymphony-orchestrator -p opensymphony-openhands

commit.message:
fix(openhands): normalize relative local tooling paths

Summary:
- canonicalize local OpenHands tooling directories before building the
  supervised launch command
- add supervisor coverage for launching from a relative tool_dir path
- document the repo-relative live doctor launch behavior in ops docs

Rationale:
- the checked-in live doctor config points openhands.tool_dir at a
  repo-relative path, and the supervisor previously passed that same
  relative run-local.sh path to bash after changing cwd into the tool
  dir, which broke the required live validation path
- normalizing the tool path once keeps the pinned launcher usable from
  repo configs without depending on the caller cwd

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test -p opensymphony-openhands --test supervisor supervised_start_supports_relative_tool_dir_paths -- --nocapture
- cargo test --workspace
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets

commit.message:
feat(openhands): harden remote agent-server transport auth

Summary:
- accept remote OpenHands transport config, websocket auth settings,
  and path-prefixed base URLs during workflow resolution
- wire workflow-driven HTTP and WebSocket auth plus transport
  diagnostics through the runtime, session metadata, and control plane
- harden external readiness probing, local-supervisor eligibility,
  and add docs plus live pinned-server auth coverage

Rationale:
- remote agent-server mode should be a configuration change rather than
  a separate code path or local-only assumption
- pinned 1.14.0 auth behavior differs between REST and WebSocket, so
  the client and diagnostics need to model that explicitly
- operators need server/auth metadata in persisted manifests and the
  control plane to debug remote connectivity without daemon internals

Tests:
- cargo fmt
- cargo test -p opensymphony-workflow -p opensymphony-openhands -p opensymphony-domain -p opensymphony-control -p opensymphony-cli
- cargo clippy -p opensymphony-workflow -p opensymphony-openhands -p opensymphony-domain -p opensymphony-control -p opensymphony-cli --tests -- -D warnings
- OPENSYMPHONY_LIVE_OPENHANDS=1 cargo test -p opensymphony-openhands --test live_pinned_server

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix staging deploy after core dependency sync

Allow the Cloudflare Pages deploy workflow to run via workflow_dispatch so dependency sync updates can deploy the branch they modify.

Grant the sync workflow permission to dispatch actions and trigger deploy.yml explicitly after it pushes a dependency bump, avoiding GitHub's suppression of follow-up workflow runs from GITHUB_TOKEN pushes.

Document the deployment behavior so the setup notes match the actual trigger path used by automated sync updates.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix presence subscription update loops

Stabilize presence observers across PresenceUsers, BringToMe, and UserTracking to prevent repeated subscriptions from driving React into a maximum update depth error.

Use a stable presence subject per effect, initialize state from the current presence snapshot when the effect mounts, and unsubscribe from the same captured subject during cleanup.

In PresenceUsers, guard user, follower, and tracked-user state updates so unchanged presence payloads do not trigger redundant rerenders.

Verification: bun run build completed the production build step, but the repository still has pre-existing TypeScript errors in unrelated areas during the type-generation phase.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix duplicate nested item indexing

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: suppress stale signals from triggering heartbeat delivery (#32)

* fix: suppress stale signals from triggering heartbeat delivery

Two fixes for the heartbeat decision pipeline:

1. Filter expired goal_progress: Plans with days_left < 0 and status
   "stalled" or "at_risk" are now excluded from active signals. Only
   expired plans with status "on_track" (active work despite deadline)
   survive the filter.

2. Suppress low-tier-only signals without confluence: When the highest
   signal tier is "low" (patterns, sentiment, relationships, decaying)
   and the combined weight doesn't meet the confluence threshold, the
   engine now returns should_act=false instead of falling through to act.

Also gitignore demo-snapshots.json and *.db.hnsw test artifacts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(heartbeat): preserve no-expiry goal progress

---------

Implement hierarchy navigation UI for groups and frames

Fold Langfuse context spans into turn metadata (#104)

* fix(BUB-134): fold langfuse context spans into turns

Summary:
- fold Langfuse context snapshot, boundary, and attribution events
  into the parent turn attributes inside backend-langfuse
- add adapter, unit, golden, and BDD coverage for folded Langfuse
  output while preserving canonical export-json context events
- record reusable test-runner patterns and implementation notes in
  progress.txt

Rationale:
- remove standalone context spans from Langfuse traces without
  changing canonical event generation or report aggregation
- keep the behavior isolated to the Langfuse adapter so other
  backends and telemetry contracts remain unchanged

Tests:
- npm run check
- npm test
- npm run telemetry:check
- npm run test:bdd

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(BUB-134): address review follow-ups

Summary:
- preserve context events that cannot be folded and add regression
  coverage for unknown and orphan context events
- strengthen the hook BDD assertion and switch the new CLI unit
  test to the package import for otlp-core
- remove progress.txt from version control while keeping it local and
  ignored for future iterations

Rationale:
- avoid silently dropping future Langfuse context event types and keep
  review feedback changes scoped to correctness and test boundaries
- align the branch with the repo's existing progress.txt ignore policy

* fix(BUB-134): preserve mixed context events in langfuse folding

Summary:
- only drop context events that match the known foldable event types
  when a turn has folded Langfuse context metadata
- avoid mutating folded summary attrs in place and tighten the mixed
  context regression coverage in backend-langfuse tests

Rationale:
- prevent mixed known-plus-unknown context events for the same turn
  from losing the unknown event during Langfuse adapter folding
- close the remaining review gap without expanding scope beyond the
  adapter and its regression tests

Tests:

commit.message:
Add Mathlib naming notes and repo audit

Write two artifact notes on Mathlib naming conventions and the current naming debt in the Bridgeland stability repo.

The first note records the corrected naming rules that matter here, including the distinction between theorem-style proof names and proposition-like named objects, the role of namespaces, the treatment of embedded UpperCamelCase concepts inside proof names, and the implementation split between proof-only subtypes and richer bundled wrappers.

The second note applies those conventions to a fresh audit of the repository. It corrects the earlier overbroad casing pass, identifies the highest-priority rename targets, records the design-linked naming problem around NumericalStabilityCondition, and separates immediate theorem-name fixes from larger abbreviation-family cleanups.

No Lean code changes are included in this commit; it adds documentation artifacts only.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Reject ambiguous decimal/string unions

Reject property ranges that mix xsd:string and xsd:decimal during
code generation, since both map to runtime strings and the generated
encoder cannot disambiguate them reliably.

Update the vocab-tools tests to cover the rejection path directly,
and document the restriction in the manual and changelog.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/640#discussion_r2971801567

commit.message:
fix: heartbeat system overhaul — 10 fixes (#29)

* fix: heartbeat system overhaul — signals_only, confidence gating, tag tiers, stability weighting

Critical fix: add signals_only mode so plugin gets fresh signals after watcher
already decided to act, instead of re-running decision pipeline and hitting
cooldown. Also fixes 9 additional heartbeat issues:

- Add CheckPositiveDeltas/CheckMemoryVelocity to allChecks, gate computation
- Stale escape re-acts with real signals instead of always falling to nudge
- PendingWork recency gate: filter plans older than 7d without upcoming deadline
- Topic dedup window uses SignalCooldownNormal instead of hardcoded 1h
- Confidence gating: exclude low-confidence memories from signals (default 0.5)
- Tag-based tier adjustment: critical/urgent boost, low-priority/backlog demote
- Stability weighting in confluence scoring
- Response rate multiplier uses smooth linear interpolation instead of step function
- Remove duplicate comment block

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: exclude expired pending-work deadlines

* fix: record stale-escape acts

* fix(heartbeat): preserve signals_only signals

- teach extension studio to validate package paths first and switch between
  install and update actions from the backend lifecycle plan
- refresh packaged MCP connector updates, dedupe same-id overrides in the
  extension list, and document the shipped version/update behavior

Rationale:
- package evolution needs first-class update semantics instead of overloading
  install or relying on UI heuristics
- workspace overrides and packaged connector updates must behave consistently in
  runtime, API, and studio surfaces to avoid hidden drift

Tests:
- PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache python3 -m py_compile backend/src/api/extensions.py backend/src/api/activity.py backend/src/extensions/lifecycle.py backend/src/extensions/registry.py backend/src/tools/mcp_manager.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_extensions_api.py
- cd frontend && npm test -- src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: add item attribute syntax (#17)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Top-level attributes supported: [[name]] and [[name = "value"]] on
structs, enums, functions, and extern items; attributes are stored on
declarations and shown in formatted output (with proper string
escaping).
* **Lexer**
* '[' and ']' are recognized as distinct tokens to enable attribute
syntax.
* **Tests**
* Added parser, printer, and error tests covering attribute parsing,
rendering, escaping, and edge cases.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
ci: fast release pipeline — auto-tag, CI fast path, release script (#72)

- Add auto-tag-release.yml: detects chore(release): vX.Y.Z on main,
  validates against Cargo.toml, creates+pushes tag via PAT so release.yml
  triggers without manual handoff
- CI fast path: skip heavy jobs (clippy, MSRV, docs, cargo-deny, gitleaks,
  browser-scripts, 3-OS builds) for release-only PRs that touch only
  Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock/CHANGELOG.md. Keeps fmt + test as fast blockers.
- Remove changelog CI job (release notes generated in release.yml)
- Add scripts/release.sh: one-command release prep (bump, check, commit,
  push, PR)
- Document new flow in CLAUDE.md

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(extensions): surface lifecycle activity in audit and ledger

Summary:
- log extension validation, save, install, enable, disable, configure, and remove actions with richer lifecycle details, including failure cases
- teach the Activity Ledger to render extension lifecycle audit events as first-class extension entries instead of generic integration noise
- document the new lifecycle visibility and mark the audit/activity roadmap slice complete

Rationale:
- operators need to see extension changes and blocked lifecycle actions in the same accountability surfaces as the rest of Seraph activity
- the existing integration audit seam was already the right source of truth, but it needed extension-specific metadata and ledger presentation
- author docs should explain that extension lifecycle actions are now visible in audit and the Activity Ledger

commit.message:
feat(extensions): enforce package permissions and approvals

Summary:
- add manifest permission evaluation for packaged skills, workflows, and connectors, and surface the resulting approval profile through the extension lifecycle APIs
- keep permission-invalid packaged skills and workflows out of the active runtime, and block degraded packages from being re-enabled until validation issues are fixed
- require lifecycle approval for high-risk installs/enables using fingerprints bound to the validated package path and content digest, with regression coverage and doc updates

Rationale:
- make extension permissions enforceable at install and enable time instead of treating manifests as advisory metadata
- prevent approval replay against changed local packages and keep degraded packages from consuming operator approvals they cannot satisfy
- document the real lifecycle behavior so package authors know when validation failures hard-block install versus leave installed packages repairable

Tests:
- PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache python3 -m py_compile backend/src/api/extensions.py backend/src/extensions/lifecycle.py backend/src/extensions/permissions.py backend/src/extensions/registry.py backend/src/extensions/doctor.py backend/src/skills/manager.py backend/src/workflows/manager.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_extension_doctor.py tests/test_extensions_api.py tests/test_skills.py tests/test_workflows.py
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check
- reviewer subagent: no findings after re-review
